text: Continuous Integration (CI) is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each change triggers a CI pipeline, which automatically builds the new code and runs a set of tests. The key benefit of CI is catching issues early, before they become larger problems. Automated builds ensure the code compiles correctly, while running tests checks for bugs or errors. If a test fails, the team is notified immediately so they can fix the issue. This process helps maintain code quality and saves time by making debugging easier.
